title: hieracium neomarginatum f. chaetocephalum
intro: Hieracium neomarginatum f. chaetocephalum, also known as a variant of the hawkweed plant, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium neomarginatum f. chaetocephalum include its potential to reduce inflammation and combat infections, as well as its traditional use in treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
